task_completion.description: The agent accessed the OpenMRS Administration page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the OpenMRS Administration page or the OpenMRS login screen at https://localhost/openmrs/admin/index.htm.

task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the Patient Identifier Types management section or the form to add a new identifier type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A page titled 'Manage Patient Identifier Types' or 'Add Patient Identifier Type' within the OpenMRS interface.

task_completion.description: The agent entered the correct name for the new Patient Identifier Type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'National Health ID' entered into the 'Name' input field on the Add Patient Identifier Type form.

task_completion.description: The agent entered the correct description for the new Patient Identifier Type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Unique national health identifier issued by the Ministry of Health for the Health Information Exchange program' entered into the 'Description' input field.

task_completion.description: The agent saved the new Patient Identifier Type and it was successfully created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message indicating the identifier type was saved, or 'National Health ID' appearing in the list of existing Patient Identifier Types.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the OpenMRS GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the OpenMRS interface, rather than terminal windows executing curl commands or API requests.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenMRS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI elements, styling, and URL bar (showing https://localhost/openmrs/...) match a genuine OpenMRS web application, not a text editor or mock HTML file.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the GUI to insert the record directly into the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of database client interfaces (like MySQL CLI or phpMyAdmin) being used to run INSERT queries for the identifier type.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The OpenMRS admin interface is accessed via https://localhost/openmrs/admin/index.htm. The standard default credentials are username: 'superman', password: 'Admin123'. The task requires creating a Patient Identifier Type with the exact name 'National Health ID' and a description containing the keywords: unique, national, health, ministry, exchange.
